The easiest way to get around France is with an organised trip. Here at Flights and Packages, we have some great value France packages to different parts of the country.

How long is the flight to France from the UK?

It depends on which part of France you fly into. A direct flight to Paris from the UK takes around an hour. A flight to the South of France will take about double that.

Which flight operators travel to France?

There is an abundant selection of flight operators who fly to different parts of France from the UK. These include Air France, British Airways, EasyJet, Flybe and Ryanair.

Do I need a visa to visit France?

Please visit the FCO website for France entry requirements.

posts.title: Greece - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where do you fly into for a Greek island hopping holiday?

It depends on the group of islands and your specific tour. For the Cyclades, you would likely fly into Athens, Santorini or Mykonos. The Dodecanese would be Kos or Rhodes. The Sporades would be Skiathos and the Ionian islands would be Zante.

Can you island hop from Santorini?

Yes. Santorini has good ferry connections and can be combined with holidays to Crete, Mykonos, Paros and Naxos.

How long is the flight to Greece from the UK?

The average flight to Greece is around 3 hours and 20 minutes. 

Which is the best Greek island to visit?

It depends on your taste as they all have fantastic qualities, which is why Greek island hopping holidays are a great option. The most popular Greek islands are Crete, Kos, Santorini, Rhodes, Mykonos, Zante and Kos. Less touristy islands include Lesbos, Kefalonia, Ithaca, Naxos and Ios – plus, many more.

How do I get from Athens to Santorini?

There are regular flights between Athens and Santorini that take 50 minutes. 

How many Greek islands are there in total?

Greece has 6,000 islands in total, of which 227 are inhabited.

Do I need a visa to visit Greece?

Please visit the FCO website for Greece entry requirements.

posts.title: Helsinki -Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Helsinki?

Helsinki is the capital and the most populous city of Finland.

How long is the flight to Helsinki from the UK?

A direct flight to Helsinki from the UK takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes.

Is Helsinki expensive?

Finland has a reputation for being an expensive country, however, it’s quite easy to find a cheap Helsinki city break.

What is there to do in Helsinki?

Helsinki is a capital city that boasts plenty to see and do. Popular attractions include Sea Fortress Suomenlinna, Temppeliaukio Church, Old Market Hall, Helsinki Cathedral and Ateneum Art Museum.

Do I need a visa to visit Helsinki?

Please visit the FCO website for Finland entry requirements.

posts.title: India - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
What cities are in India’s Golden Triangle?

A Golden Triangle holiday visits Delhi, Agra and Jaipur.

When should I visit the Golden Triangle?

Generally, the best time to visit the Golden Triangle is October - April, where temperatures are comfortable. Monsoon season runs from July - September. 

How long is the Golden Triangle?

The Golden Triangle circuit is around 450 miles in total, with each leg around a 4 to6-hour drive.

What is the meaning of the Golden Triangle?

The Golden Triangle gets its name from the triangular shape formed by the locations of New Delhi, Agra and Jaipur on a map. 

Is it safe to travel to India?

In general, travelling around India is considered safe. The tourist industry is growing massively and holidays to India are increasingly popular with UK tourists.

Do I need a visa to travel to India?

Please visit the FCO website for India entry requirements.

What is the currency of India?

The Indian rupee is the currency of India. However, tourists are not permitted to bring any Indian currency into the country.

posts.title: Ischia - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Ischia?

Ischia is a volcanic island in the Tyrrhenian Sea that belongs to Italy. It’s located around 19 miles from the city of Naples.

Is Ischia a good holiday destination?

Yes, Ischia is a popular tourist destination and holidays in Ischia offer a beautiful blend of culture, leisure and history.

What is Ischia famous for?

As well as a famous holiday destination, Ischia is known for the legendary healing properties of its thermal springs.

How do I get to Ischia?

The closest airport to Ischia is Naples and a direct flight from the UK takes 2 hours and 45 minutes. There are regular ferries from Naples to Ischia that take around an hour.

Do I need a visa to visit Ischia?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: Italy - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Is it expensive in Italy?

Italy does not have to be expensive. Package Italy holidays often offer very good value and can include meals. Rural locations in Italy tend to be very affordable, however, some Italian cities such as Rome, Florence and Venice can have expensive areas, usually in the city centre. 

What are the main holiday destinations in Italy and where should I visit?

Italy is awash with fantastic destinations, whether you want a city break, a multi-stop tour, or a beach holiday. Popular places include Rome, Florence, Sorrento, Sicily, Venice, Tuscany, Siena, Milan, Lake Garda, Lake Como, Verona, Cinque Terra - and there are plenty more.

Is Italy hot in October?

Italy has pleasant temperatures in October with Milan and Rome averaging around 18-20°C. In southern Italy, temperatures range from 18-25°C.

Do I need a visa to visit Italy?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

How do I travel around Japan?

As there is so much to see and do, the easiest way to get around is with an organised tour of Japan. 

When is the best time to visit Japan?

The most popular times to visit Japan are from late March to early April (cherry blossom season) and during Autumn (September to November).

How long is the flight to Japan from the UK?

A direct flight from the UK to Tokyo takes around 13 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Japan?

Please visit the FCO website for Japan entry requirements.

What is the currency of Japan?

The Japanese yen is the official currency of Japan.

posts.title: Kenya - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Is Kenya safe for a holiday?

Kenya is generally considered safe and has one of the most developed tourism sectors in Africa. However, travelling as part of an organised tour in Kenya is safer than independent travel.

What are the best places to visit in Kenya?

Kenya has many highlights and is a popular choice for beach holidays as well as safaris. Tourist destinations include Nairobi, Mombasa, Maasai Game Reserve, Lake Nakuru National Park, Samburu, Lake Naivasha, Lamu, Tsavo, Amboseli and Mount Kenya.

Is English spoken in Kenya?

Kenyans who work in the tourism industry usually speak good English, but the main language is Kiswahili.

What is food like in Kenya?

Most of the time you’ll eat in your hotel and lodge, where western cuisine, as well as local dishes, are served. In major cities, there are many restaurants to choose from, and by the coast the fresh seafood is excellent. 

How long is the flight to Kenya from the UK?

A direct flight from the UK to Kenya takes around 8 and a half hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Kenya?

Please visit the FCO website for Kenya entry requirements.

What is the currency of Kenya?

The local currency is the Kenya Shilling.

posts.title: Lake Como - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Lake Como?

Lake Como, also known as Lario, is a beautiful lake of glacial origin located in Lombardy, in Northern Italy.

What is there to do in Lake Como?

As well as beautiful natural scenery, Lake Como has plenty of historic sites and walking trails. As Milan is one of the nearest cities to Lake Como, twin centre holidays to Milan and Lake Como are a popular choice with tourists.

Do I need a visa to visit Lake Como?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: Lake Garda - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Lake Garda?

Lake Garda is the largest lake in Italy and a popular holiday destination in northern Italy.

What is there to do in Lake Garda?

Lake Garda is most famous for its beautiful natural scenery. A holiday to Lake Garda offers a romantic and relaxing trip with stunning views and historical sites.

When is the best time to visit Lake Garda?

The summer months of July and August are the best time to visit Lake Garda, although spring is a wonderful time to visit too.

Do I need a visa to visit Lake Garda?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.content:
What is there to do Lisbon?

Lisbon has plenty of things to do. Some of the highlights of a Lisbon city break include the nearby beaches, the historic trams and the art museums. Lisbon is also famous for its wonderful cuisine.

When is the best time to visit Lisbon?

The best time to visit Lisbon is generally considered to be in spring and summer (April – September).

How long is the flight to Lisbon from the UK?

A direct flight to Lisbon from the UK takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes.

Do I need a visa to visit Lisbon?

Please visit the FCO website for Portugal entry requirements.

posts.title: Ljubljana - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Ljubljana?

Ljubljana is the capital and largest city of Slovenia. 

How long is the flight to Ljubljana from the UK?

A direct flight to Ljubljana from the UK takes around 2 hours and 5 minutes.

What is there to do in Ljubljana?

Ljubljana has plenty to see and do and was also crowned the Green Capital of Europe. Popular attractions include Ljubljana Old Town, Tivoli Park, Preseren Square, River Ljubljanica Kanal, Ljubljana Castle and the Cathedral of St. Nicholas.

Is Ljubljana expensive?

Ljubljana is an affordable city and enjoying a break to Ljubljana is fast becoming a popular choice with UK tourists.

Do I need a visa to visit Ljubljana?

Please visit the FCO website for Slovenia entry requirements.

posts.title: Madeira - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Madeira?

Madeira is a Portuguese island in the Atlantic, west of the Mediterranean. It has the nickname ‘the Garden Island’ due to its lush scenery and it also boasts beautiful weather.

When is the best time to visit Madeira?

The best time to take a Madeira holiday is generally considered to be from April to November.

How long is the flight to Madeira from the UK?

A direct flight to Madeira from the UK takes around 3 hours and 55 minutes.

Do I need a visa to visit Madeira?

Please visit the FCO website for Portugal entry requirements.

posts.title: Maldives - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is the Maldives?

The Maldives is a South Asian country and an island archipelago located in the Indian Ocean, southwest of Sri Lanka and India.

Why should I visit the Maldives?

Luxurious hotels, stunning white-sand beaches, amazing snorkelling and diving opportunities make the Maldives a clear choice for a holiday of a lifetime.

When is the best time to visit the Maldives?

The Maldives stays wonderfully warm year-round, and showers are usually very short during the wet season. However, the dry season (January – April) is considered the peak time to visit.

Is it expensive to holiday in the Maldives?

The Maldives often gets the reputation of being an expensive holiday destination, but there are inexpensive options available. Here at Flights and Packages, we have some incredibly affordable Maldives holidays.

How long is the flight to Maldives from the UK?

A direct flight to Male from the UK takes around 10 and a half hours. You will then need to transfer to your specific island - usually by speedboat. 

Do I need a visa to visit Maldives?

Please visit the FCO website for Maldives entry requirements.

posts.title: Marrakech - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Marrakech located?

Marrakech is a major city and popular tourist destination in Morocco. It is located at the foothills of the Atlas Mountains, just a few hours away from the Sahara Desert.

When is the best time to visit Marrakech?

The best time to visit Marrakech (for warm, comfortable temperatures) is from March-May and September - November. The cooler winter months of December and February are also popular with tourists and the weather can still reach 70°F. June and August can get very hot with temperatures of 95°F.

What is there to do in Marrakech?

Marrakech has plenty to see and do. The historic Medina (Jemaa el-Fnaa), Koutoubia Mosque, Majorelle Garden, an array of speciality museums and desert safaris are just a handful of the highlights.

What are the hotels like in Marrakech?

There are traditional Riad hotels located in the centre of Marrakech. However, many tourists opt to stay outside of central Marrakech and there are some luxury resorts with top facilities on the outskirts of the city. All inclusive holidays to Marrakech are therefore increasingly popular.

Is Marrakech safe for a holiday?

Marrakech is considered a very safe city for tourists.

How long is the flight to Marrakechfrom the UK?

A direct flight to Marrakech from the UK takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes.

Do I need a visa to visit Marrakech?

Please visit the FCO website for Morocco entry requirements.

What is the currency inMarrakech?

The local currency is the Moroccan dirham.

posts.title: Mauritius - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Mauritius?

Mauritius is an island nation in the Indian Ocean, around 1,300 miles off the south-east coast of Africa. 

Where are the best beaches in Mauritius?

Mauritius has over 100 miles of beautiful beaches, so there are plenty to choose from. Some of the most popular beaches include Grand Baie, Trou aux Biches, Île aux Cerfs, Belle Mare Plage, Flic en Flac and Le Morne.

When is the best time to visit Mauritius?

Mauritius stays warm year-round, but the most popular months to visit are from October – April.

Is it expensive to holiday in Mauritius?

Mauritius is often considered an expensive holiday destination, but there are inexpensive options available. Here at Flights and Packages, we have a collection of affordable Mauritius holidays.

How long is the flight to Mauritius from the UK?

A direct flight from the UK takes around 12 hours. 

Do I need a visa to visit Mauritius?

Please visit the FCO website for Mauritius entry requirements.

posts.title: Mexico - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
When is the best time of year to visit Mexico?

The best time to visit Mexico is from January - April, when the weather is dry and coastal resorts average 24°C. The wet season runs from June - November, however, showers tend to happen in the late afternoon and don’t last very long, so tourists still travel to Mexico during these months. 

Where are the best beaches in Mexico?

The beach resort of Cancun is widely considered one of the most beautiful coastal resorts in Mexico, with many tourists opting to enjoy a holiday in Cancun with all inclusive board. Cozumel, Playa del Carmen, Tulum, Puerto Vallarta and Cabo San Lucas are also popular choices.

Is Mexico safe?

Mexico is a very safe country to visit as long as you keep in mind some common-sense safety tips.

How long is the flight to Mexico from the UK?

Direct flights to Mexico City take around 11 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Mexico?

Please visit the FCO website for Mexico entry requirements.

What is the currency of Mexico?

The local currency is the Mexican peso.

posts.title: Montenegro - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Montenegro?

Montenegro is a country in Southeast Europe on the Adriatic Sea.

What is Montenegro famous for?

Montenegro is famous for offering beautiful beach holidays. Package holidays to Montenegro have become increasingly popular with tourists over the last decade and it offers a fantastic alternative to some of the more popular European destinations.

When is the best time to visit Montenegro?

For sunny beach weather, the best time to visit Montenegro is between April and September.

How long is the flight to Montenegrofrom the UK?

Direct flights to Montenegro from the UK take around 3 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Montenegro?

Please visit the FCO website for Montenegro entry requirements.

posts.title: Nepal - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Is Nepal safe for a holiday?

Nepalis considered a very safe country to holiday in and locals are very welcoming towards tourists.

When is the best time to visit Nepal?

The best time to visit Nepal is from December - April. Tourists tend to avoid the monsoon season, which usually lasts from June until the end of September.

How do I travel around Nepal?

The easiest way to get around is with an organised Nepal tour, which includes transfers to the country’s hotspots.

How long is the flight to Nepal from the UK?

An indirect flight to Kathmandu from the UK usually takes around 14 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Nepal?

Please visit the FCO website for Nepal entry requirements.

What is the currency of Nepal?

The Nepalese rupee is the official currency of Nepal.

posts.title: Panama - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Panama?

Panama is a country in Central America that shares a border with Costa Rica and has coastlines on the Caribbean Sea and the Pacific Ocean. Due to their proximity, many tourists choose to go on a twin-centre holiday to Panama and Costa Rica.

What is Panama famous for?

Although Panama is known for its beautiful beaches and lush rain forests, it is primarily famous for the Panama Canal.

What are the best places to visit in Panama?

Some of the best places to visit in Panama include Panama City, San Blas Islands, Chiriqui Cloud forest, Boquete and Bocas del Toro.

How long is the flight to Panama?

Flights to Panama take around 14 hours in total (with one-stop).

Is Panama safe for a holiday?

Panama is generally considered a safe country, but you should take the usual precautions on the streets of major cities, especially after dark. 

Do I need a visa to visit Panama?

Please visit the FCO website for Panama entry requirements.

What is the currency in Panama?

The currency used day-to-day in Panama is the US dollar.

posts.content:
Where is Paphos?

Paphos is a coastal city and a popular holiday destination in the southwest of Cyprus.

What kind of holidays are popular in Paphos?

Paphos is famous for package beach holidays and the area is a popular choice for an all inclusive Cyprus holiday. As well as its wonderful beaches, Paphos is also blessed with an array of historical sites.

How long is the flight to Paphos from the UK?

A direct flight to Paphos takes around 4 hours and 45 minutes.

Do I need a visa to visit Paphos?

Please visit the FCO website for Cyprus entry requirements.

posts.title: Peru - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
What are the main holiday destinations in Peru?

Popular destinations in Peru include Machu Picchu, Cusco, Arequipa, Lima, Punos, the Sacred Valley and Mancora. 

Is Peru safe?

Yes, it is generally considered safe, especially when visited on an organised Peru tour.

Where does it snow in Peru?

It only snows in the Andes during the winter season.

Do British Airways fly to Peru?

Yes, British Airways have both direct and indirect flights to the capital city, Lima. 

How long is the flight to Peru from the UK?

Non-stop flights to Lima take around 12.5 hours, and indirect flights are usually around 17 hours. 

Do I need a visa to visit Peru?

Please visit the FCO website for Peru entry requirements.

What is the currency of Peru?

The Peruvian nuevo sol is the local currency but US dollars are also widely accepted.

posts.title: Poland - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where are popular places to visit in Poland?

City breaks in Poland are a popular choice with UK tourists, with Warsaw (the capital) and Krakow, two of the most popular destinations.

How long is the flight to Poland from the UK?

A direct flight to Poland from the UK takes around 2 and a half hours.

What is Polish food like?

Poland is known for hearty, comfort food. Popular ingredients include meat, sauerkraut, mushrooms, seasonal fruit, sweet cottage cheese and boiled potatoes with fried onions (called Russian dumplings). Poland is also famous for its beer which is very affordable.

Do I need a visa to visit Poland?

Please visit the FCO website for Poland entry requirements.

posts.title: Portugal - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
What is Portugal famous for?

As well as offering a wide array of different holidays, Portugal is famous for port wine, football and its delicious custard tarts.

Do they speak English in Portugal?

Portuguese is the official language in Portugal, however many locals who work in the hospitality industry speak English.

What are the most popular destinations in Portugal?

Portugal offers an array of fantastic city breaks, beach holidays and island escapes. Due to the variety, holidays to Portugal are incredibly popular with UK tourists.

Do I need a visa to visit Portugal?

Please visit the FCO website for Portugal entry requirements.

posts.title: Prague - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Prague?

Prague is the largest city in the Czech Republic and its capital. 

How long is the flight to Prague from the UK?

A direct flight to Prague from the UK takes around 2 hours.

Is Prague expensive?

Cheap city breaks in Prague are widely available and food and drink is generally very affordable, once there.

What is there to do in Prague?

Prague has plenty to see and do. Popular attractions include Prague Castle, Charles Square, the Old Town Square, Prague City Museum and Prague Astronomical Clock.

Do I need a visa to visit Prague?

Please visit the FCO website for Czech Republic entry requirements.

posts.title: Puglia - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Puglia?

Puglia is a region in Southern Italy located on the heel of Italy’s boot. Due to its location, Puglia boasts fantastic weather, with holidays and tours to Puglia proving very popular with UK tourists.

Which is the best beach to stay at in Puglia?

Puglia has stunning beaches. Some of the most beautiful include Pescoluse, the Tremiti Islands, Porto Badisco and Torre Guaceto.

When is the best time to visit Puglia?

The best months for good weather in Puglia are from April - October.

Do I need a visa to visit Puglia?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: Ras Al Khaimah - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Ras Al Khaimah?

Ras Al Khaimah is one of seven emirates that make up the United Arab Emirates (UAE). It is the northern-most emirate of the UAE and now a popular tourist destination located only a 60-minute drive from Dubai.

When is the best time to visit Ras Al Khaimah?

The best time to visit Ras Al Khaimah is during the winter season, from November to February, when the weather is sunny and comfortable. During the height of summer (June to August), the temperature can reach over 45°c.

Is Ras Al Khaimah safe for a holiday?

Yes. Ras Al Khaimah is generally considered very safe and is often described as a slower-paced version of Dubai.

Is Ras Al Khaimah a good destination for an all inclusive holiday?

Taking an all inclusive holiday in Ras A Khaimah has become a popular choice for UK citizens due to the year-round warm weather, luxurious hotels and beautiful beaches.

How long is the flight to Ras Al Khaimah from the UK?

A direct flight to Ras Al Khaimah takes around 7 hours and 45 minutes from London. 

Do I need a visa to visit Ras Al Khaimah?

Please visit the FCO website for the United Arab Emirates entry requirements.

What is the currency of Ras Al Khaimah?

The local currency in Ras Al Khaimah is the United Arab Emirates Dirham.

posts.title: Riga - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Riga?

Riga is the capital of Latvia and a popular destination for a city break.

What is there to do Riga?

Riga has plenty of interesting attractions including the Old City, Riga Motormuseum, the Central Market, St Peter’s Church Tower and the Latvian Ethnographic Open-Air Museum.

What is Riga famous for?

Riga is famous for beautiful Art Nouveau architecture and it boasts one of the world’s highest concentration of Art Nouveau structures. It’s this architecture combined with cultural attractions and affordability that makes it a popular destinations with tourists on a Riga city break.

How long is the flight to Riga from the UK?

A direct flight to Riga from the UK takes around 2 hours and 45 minutes.

Do I need a visa to visit Riga?

Please visit the FCO website for Latvia entry requirements.

posts.title: Rome - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
When is the best time to visit Rome?

Most tourists visit Rome during the warmer months (April - September). However, winter is also a fantastic time to visit this fascinating city.

What is Rome famous for?

Rome is famous for its rich history, art and numerous fountains. It’s also renowned for fantastic food and its romantic atmosphere - many couples from around the world embark on a break to Rome.

How long is the flight to Rome from the UK?

A direct flight to Rome from the UK takes around 2 and a half hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Rome?

Please visit the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

Where in Italy is Sardinia?

Sardinia is an Italian island and the second-largest island in the Mediterranean Sea. It is located west of the Italian Peninsula.

When is the best time to visit Sardinia?

The best time to holiday in Sardinia is during spring and summer (from late April to September).

Do I need a visa to visit Sardinia?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: Sicily - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Sicily?

Sicily is an Italian island that is separated from the south eastern tip of the mainland by a 2-mile long bridge.

Is it safe to visit Sicily?

Yes, it is safe for tourists and millions of visitors enjoy a holiday in Sicily every year.

Which is the best beach to stay at in Sicily?

Sicily has a plethora of beautiful beaches. Popular resorts include Mondello, Cefalu, Isola Bella beach, Giardini Naxos, Fontane Bianche and Scala Dei Turchi.

Which airlines fly to Sicily from the UK?

British Airways, EasyJet, Norwegian Air, TUI, Air Malta and Ryanair all fly direct from London to Sicily. 

Do I need a visa to visit Sicily?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: Sorrento - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where in Italy is Sorrento?

Sorrento is a seaside resort in south-west Italy, located about 30 miles from Naples.

Is Sorrento a good holiday destination?

Yes, Sorrento is a popular tourist destination due to its charming atmosphere and picturesque location on the Amalfi Coast, close to Naples and Pompeii.

What is Sorrento famous for?

As well as a famous holiday destination, Sorrento is known for its delicious lemons, a local variety that is sweeter and more succulent than regular lemons.

Which is the best part of Sorrento to stay in?

The best part of Sorrento to stay in is either in the centre, Piazza Tasso or at one of the nearby beach hotels.

What is there to do in Sorrento?

As well as relaxing on the beach and sampling delicious local cuisine, cooking classes, boat excursions and exploring the pretty main square are also popular pastimes during holidays in Sorrento.

Do I need a visa to visit Sorrento?

Please see the FCO website for Italy entry requirements.

posts.title: South Africa - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Is South Africa safe for a holiday?

Yes, it’s generally considered safe for tourists in South Africa. However, it's wise to avoid entering townships or neighbourhoods with bad reputations, unless accompanied by a guide who knows the area well.

How do I travel around South Africa?

The easiest way to get around is with an organised trip. A package tour of South Africa usually includes the country’s best destinations and plenty of excursions via hassle-free transfers.

What are the best parks for safaris in South Africa?

There are many to choose from. Some of the best parks for spotting wildlife are the Kruger National Park, Kgalagadi Transfrontier Park, Addo Elephant National Park, Mapungubwe National Park and Hluhluwe-Umfolozi Game Reserve.

What are the best beaches in South Africa?

South Africa has a huge collection of stunning beaches. Some of the most scenic include Camps Bay and Clifton in Cape Town, Boulders Beach near Cape Town (home to penguins), Mossel Bay, JeffreysBay, Port St. Johns, Plettenberg Bay and Thonga Beach which is located in Isimangaliso World Heritage Park.

How long is the flight to South Africa from the UK?

It depends on which part of South Africa you fly into. A direct flight to Cape Town from the UK takes around 11 and a half hours. Johannesburg is slightly less at 11 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit South Africa?

Please visit the FCO website for South Africa entry requirements.

What is the currency of South Africa?

The rand is the official currency of South Africa.

posts.title: Sri Lanka - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Is it safe to visit Sri Lanka?

Despite the tragic events in April 2019, Sri Lanka is a safe country to visit. Package tours and holidays to Sri Lanka remain a popular choice with UK tourists.

When should I go on holiday to Sri Lanka?

The weather in Sri Lanka is quite complex. Generally, the best time to visit central Sri Lanka, or the west and south coast is between December and April. On the east coast the climate is at its finest from May to October.

How long is the flight to Sri Lankafrom the UK?

Direct flights to Sri Lanka take an average time of 11 hours.

Do I need a visa to visit Sri Lanka?

Please visit the FCO website for Sri Lanka entry requirements.

What is the currency of Sri Lanka?

The local currency is the Sri Lankan Rupee.

posts.title: Tallinn - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Tallinn?

Tallinn is the capital and the most populous city of Estonia. 

How long is the flight to Tallinn from the UK?

A direct flight to Tallinn from the UK takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes.

Is Tallinn expensive?

It’s quite easy to find a cheap Tallinn city break and there are many affordable options when eating out in Tallinn. Local beer is also inexpensive! 

What is there to do in Tallinn?

Tallinn has plenty to see and do. Popular attractions include Kadriorg Park, the scenic Old Town, Kumu Art Museum, St Olaf’s Church, Seaplane Harbour and Kohtuotsa viewing platform.

What is the food like in Tallinn?

You’ll find plenty of options when it comes to food in Tallinn. If you want to try local delicacies then Estonian chocolate, rye bread, sprats and Mulgipuder (a mashed potato dish) are must-tries. 

Do I need a visa to visit Tallinn?

Please visit the FCO website for Estonia entry requirements.

posts.title: Thailand - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
How much is island hopping in Thailand?

An island-hopping getaway to Thailand can be extremely affordable, especially when booked at part of a package tour. We currently have Thailand island hopping holidays with flights and extras available from only £899 pp. Find out more here>

How do you get from Bangkok to the Thai islands?

There are internal flights available from Bangkok to the main Thai islands. Most holidaymakers fly from Bangkok to Phuket, where there are ferries to a collection of beautiful islands.

Which is the best island to visit in Thailand?

Thailand boasts hundreds of islands and favourites vary considerably. The most popular islands amongst tourists are Phuket, Ko Samui, the Phi Phi islands, Ko Pha-Ngan, Ko Lanta, Ko Tao and Ko Lipe.

Where should I go in Thailand for 2 weeks?

If you want to see the best of Thailand then an organised tour is wise. Choose from island hopping holidays, or explore Thailand’s incredible cities – such as Bangkok, Chiang Mai and Phuket.

Is it safe to travel in Thailand?

Thailand is a major hotspot for tourists from all around the world and is generally considered safe.

Do I need a visa to holiday in Thailand?

Please visit the FCO website for Thailand entry requirements.

How long are flights to Thailand?

A direct flight from London to Bangkok usually takes around 11 and a half hours.

What is the currency of Thailand?

The local currency is the Thai Baht.

posts.title: Turkey - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
When is the best time to travel to Turkey?

Turkey is considered a year-round destination, depending on what your itinerary is. For guaranteed sunshine on the south coast, visit in April – October (and bear in mind that from June - September the temperatures can reach mid 30°Cs).

What is there to do in Turkey aside from a beach holiday?

As well as Turkey’s beautiful beach resorts, tourists also visit for a city break in Istanbul, a tour of the country, or to explore the coast via a Turkey Gulet cruise.

How long is the flight to Turkey from the UK?

It depends on which part of Turkey you fly into. A flight to Istanbul in the north of Turkey takes around 4 hours and a flight to the coastal resorts in the south is usually about 20 minutes longer.

Do they speak English in Turkey?

The country’s official language is Turkish, but English is widely spoken, especially in the main tourist areas. Many locals who work in the hospitality industry are fluent in English due to the large number of British tourists that visit Turkey every year.

Do I need a visa to visit Turkey?

Please visit the FCO website for Turkey entry requirements.

What is the currency of Turkey?

The Turkish lira is the official currency of Turkey.

Where is Vienna?

Vienna is the largest city in Austria and its capital. It is located in north-eastern Austria, in central Europe.

posts.title: Zanzibar - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
Where is Zanzibar?

Zanzibar is an island that belongs to Tanzania and is located approximately 15 miles from the mainland.

Why should I holiday in Zanzibar?

Zanzibar boasts some of the best beaches in the world, with white sands and clear turquoise waters. As well as a collection of stunning beaches, Zanzibar is also home to Stone Town – which has a unique blend of Arab, Persian, Indian and European culture and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Is Zanzibar safe for a holiday?

The island is considered very safe for tourists. As tourism is the primary source of income in Zanzibar, the local authorities take safety very seriously.

Is a holiday to Zanzibar expensive?

It doesn’t have to be. Here at Flights and Packages, we are currently offering an all inclusive Zanzibar holiday with flights, transfers and 4-star accommodation from only £999 pp.

Do I need a visa to visit Zanzibar?

As Zanzibar is part of Tanzania, please visit the FCO website for Tanzania entry requirements.

What is the currency of Zanzibar?

The Tanzanian shilling is the currency used in Zanzibar.

posts.title: Twin Centre Holidays - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
What is a twin centre holiday?

A twin-center holiday incorporates two destinations in one holiday. For example, a Rome and Venice break or a Cambodia and Vietnam holiday.

Why should I go on a twin centre holiday?

Twin centre holidays are a fantastic way to make the most of your holiday time and combine your dream destinations in one escape. You can either skip between countries or explore different cities in one destination. 

What are the most popular twin centre holidays?

There is so much choice when it comes to twin centre holidays. Some popular options include two European cities, a South American twin centre holiday, or two destinations in Asia, such as a Bali and Lombok holiday. Many holidaymakers also opt to enjoy a city break combined with a beach holiday, often in completely different countries.

posts.title: Triple centre Holidays - Frequently Asked Questions
posts.content:
What is a triple centre holiday?

A triple centre holiday (also sometimes called a multi-centre holiday) incorporates three destinations in one holiday. For example, a Prague, Vienna and Budapest break or a Thailand, Cambodia and Vietnam holiday. 

Why should I go on a triple centre holiday?

Triple centre and multi-destination holidays are an amazingway to make the most of your holiday time and see a whole host of amazing destinations in just one trip.

What are the most popular triple centre holidays?

There is so much choice when it comes to triple and multi-centre holidays. You could opt to visit three European cities or you could enjoy a classic Golden Triangle tour in India, or go for something completely different, such as a Jakarta, Bali and Borneo holiday.

posts.title: 10 Reasons to Book a Twin or Multi Centre Holiday
posts.content:
1. Great Value

One fantastic reason to enjoy a twin centre holiday is that they offer fantastic value! You pay for one package and you get to see two or more destinations in one trip. Twin centre holidays in Europe are particularly good value, and often don’t cost much more than a single destination trip.

2. Best of Both Worlds

Maybe your partner is more a fan of beach holidays, whereas you prefer to hit the hot spots in the city. A twin centre holiday is a great way to compromise and do both on your holiday. Alternatively, you may want to tour a country and visit an array of iconic sights before unwinding on the beach, which is possible with a Sri Lanka and Maldives holiday.

3. Tick Off Two or More Countries in One Trip

A twin or multi centre holiday means you can tick more than one country off your bucket list in one go. Many organised tours will include carefully chose highlights in each destination, which means you really do get to see the most important attractions in a limited amount of time. 

4. Discover More

Rather than be limited to one hotel or destination, a twin or multi-centre holiday gives you the opportunity to discover and ultimately, see and do more. You may have always wanted to visit Rome but why not combine it with a holiday to Verona as well? Or maybe you want to tick Thailand off your bucket list but would like to see an array of destinations – an island hopping Thailand holiday may be the answer. With so many holidays to choose from, the world really is your oyster! 

5. Choose from an Array of Destinations

There is so much variety when it comes to twin and multi-centre holidays. You could choose a far-flung adventure such as an Ecuador and Galapagos tour, or a holiday to Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam, alternatively, you could visit somewhere closer to home and enjoy a twin-centre holiday in Italy or a holiday that explores Portugal.

6. Travel Iconic Routes

Many tourists opt to enjoy a classic twin or multi centre holiday, one that includes iconic attractions and UNESCO World Heritage Sites. One very popular trip is a Golden Triangle tour, which includes Delhi, Agra and Jaipur in India and an array of world-famous sights such as the Taj Mahal. Another iconic option is an Asia tour that includes the incredible Great Wall of China, such as a Beijing, Hong Kongand Tokyo tour.

7. Stress-Free Travel

On an organised twin centre or multi centre tour, you can just relax and enjoy the sights. Organised twin centre holidays include an itinerary and all of your transfers, flights and accommodation, and even sometimes meals. This means, you can just concentrate on having a good time. 

8. New Itineraries

With twin and multi centre holidays becoming increasingly popular with UK tourists, new itineraries arealways popping up.So, whether you have a specific destination in mind or you are open to any itinerary, there is sure to be something to suit.

9. Keep Things Exciting

There’s nothing worse than being bored on holiday. Although rare, sometimes a holiday may not quite live up to your expectations. With a twin or multi centre holiday there is so much packed in that you don’t have time to be bored. Also, if somewhere doesn’t quite meet your expectations, then you haven’t got to wait long before you are travelling to somewhere else! It also gives you the rare opportunity to compare neighbouring countries or various different cities on one trip.

10. Take that Holiday of a Lifetime

A far-flung twin or multi centre holiday has recently become a popular choice with honeymooners and tourists wanting to take a holiday of a lifetime. Popular holiday destinations include Asia, South America, the Caribbean and tours of South Africa.

posts.title: Top 10 Most Romantic Destinations in the World
posts.content:
Whether you’re on your honeymoon or just want a romantic holiday where you can rekindle your love, there are plenty of places around the world that are bound to set the scene. We’ve put together a selection of destinations that are ideal for Valentine’s Day, a surprise getaway, or simply for spending some time away with your special someone…

1 & 2. Verona and Venice

We couldn’t choose between these two iconic cities, so we chose both! Famous as the setting for Shakespeare’s most famous play (Romeo and Juliet), Verona is full of pretty piazzas, labyrinthine lanes, and a 13th century house boasting the ‘famous balcony’ that is said to have belonged to Juliet. Just an hour and a half’s drive from Verona lies the iconic city of Venice where you can enjoy soothing gondola rides with your loved one, get lost in the cobbled alleyways or watch a romantic opera. So, if you want a really romantic getaway then a Venice and Verona twin break may just tick all the boxes.

3. Maldives

A unique setting, luxury hotels, stunning beaches and a colourful underwater world all put the Maldives firmly on the romantic destinations list. A popular honeymoon destination, holidays in the Maldives are all about getting back to nature, unwinding and spending time with your someone special. Relaxing on the beach all day not your thing? The Maldives boasts some of the best diving and snorkelling in the world and the clear water is so warm that you don’t even need a wetsuit.

4. Bruges

Belgium’s medieval, fairy-tale town is one of Europe’s most well-preserved cities and is a honeypot for couples looking for a romantic break away. Charming cobbled streets and sunlit canals link quaint chocolate shops, pretty churches and inviting market squares. During the spring when the flowers are in bloom, Bruges is particularly scenic and it’s a good time to visit the 18th and 19th-century windmills that line the north-eastern side of the city. 

5. Zanzibar

With its white sand beaches, clear turquoise waters and laid-back atmosphere, it’s easy to see why the island of Zanzibar makes the cut in the romance stakes. This dreamy archipelago, around 40 miles from Tanzania also boasts a unique culture (expect to see trendy tribesmen on the beaches)and a fascinating blend of East Africa and Indian Ocean roots. Activities include exploring the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Stone Town, treating your senses to a spice tour, and exploring the island’s incredible marine life.For the ultimate romantic getaway, opt for an all inclusive Zanzibar escape, then spend your days relaxing on the beach, taking life easy and enjoying the island vibes.

6. Paris

France’s stylish capital with its beautiful architecture, sleek residents, rich foods and emotive art scene has long been associated with romance. Romantic outings with your loved one include taking in the wonderful views from the Eiffel Tower, enjoying a sunset cruise on the Seine, and enjoying a classic cabaret show at the Moulin Rouge. Paris is also brimming with incredible restaurants some of which offer spectacular views of Paris - perfect for a romantic dinner. 

7. Mauritius

Immaculate beaches, the chance to spot dolphins, a lush National Park and tasty fusion food make Mauritius a great choice for a romantic getaway. The island also has a plethora of luxurious beach hotels and holidays in Mauritius often involve a lot of lounging around with cocktail in-hand. For those who do want to venture beyond the beach, you can feel like a celebrity with a speedboat tour, marvel at marine-life on a glass-bottom boat, or explore the stunning Black River Gorges National Park which boasts a Certificate of Excellence on TripAdvisor. You’ll soon discover why Mauritius has been dubbed ‘paradise island’. 

8. Dominican Republic

Renowned for its beautiful beaches, stunning mountain scenery and grand colonial architecture, the Dominican Republic is a popular destination with couples. The crystal-clear waters and luxurious hotels in Punta Cana are a must-see for beach fans, and all inclusive Dominican Republic holidays are a great option for couples who want to unwind and spend some quality time together. For romantic day trips, you’ll find stunning National Parks with waterfalls, whale spotting trips and horseback riding.

9. Vietnam

With its staggering natural beauty, exotic landscapes and historic towns, there is something undoubtedly romantic about Vietnam. Some of its romantic highlights include delightful Hoi-An, home to an ancient riverside town boasting UNESCO status, and the UNESCO site of Halong Bay, where couples can cruise turquoise waters filled with hundreds of limestone islands jutting out of the water. If you want to put your feet up during your tour of Vietnam then the country also has plenty of spas - maybe unwind with a couples massage after a day of sightseeing?

10.Bali

Postcard beaches, evocative temples, a relaxed island vibe and dramatic mountain scenery, give Bali its romance. Most couples choose a beach holiday in Bali which can be combined with trips to other parts of the island, such as lush Ubud which is home to woven rice paddies and mountain backdrops. You can also enjoy an array of watersports when you’re not sipping cocktails by the pool. Balinese cuisine is equally delightful with perfectly spiced curries and mouth-watering meats - ideal for a candlelit meal on the beach with your partner.

posts.title: Best Destinations for a Safari
posts.content:
A safari holiday offers the unique opportunity to view animals in the wild and is often described as one of life’s great adventures. In Africa, safaris often revolve around spotting the ‘Big Five’ (lion, leopard, rhinoceros, elephant and cape buffalo), whereas a safari in Asia can provide the opportunity to see tigers in the wild. Safaris can also be a feature of another holiday, for example, a beach escape combined with game drives makes for a wonderful adventure. Whatever type of holiday you fancy, we’ve listed some of the best destinations in the world to take a safari, just don’t forget to take your camera!

South Africa

Home to the world-famous Kruger National Park, South Africa is renowned for its incredible safari opportunities. A must for wildlife fans on holiday in South Africa is the legendary Kruger National Park, home to the Big Five, plus many more animals including cheetahs, hippos, zebras and giraffe. The park also boasts over 500 species of bird and with over 12,000 square miles to explore, it’s entirely possible to avoid the crowds.

Kenya

Boasting the Masai Mara Game Reserve and the beautiful Lake Nakuru National Park, wildlife in Kenya has long attracted worldwide attention. The Masai Mara is the place to see the Big Five and the park also boasts hundreds of amphibians and birds. Or, if you want a safari with slightly fewer tourists, Lake Nakuru National Park has stunning mountain scenery with black and white rhinos, lions, leopards, hippopotamus and the endangered Rothschild's giraffe. For the ultimate Kenya holiday combine Kenya’s stunning beach destinations with safari drives in the National Parks.

Sri Lanka

As well as beautiful beaches, Sri Lanka is a country awash with wildlife. Beachside you can spot turtles, dolphins and whales, whereas inland the National Parks have an array of fascinating mammals to view by Jeep. Head to Yala National Park for the chance to see bears, elephants, wild boar, deer, peacocks and the elusive leopard in their natural habitat. Alternatively, Minneriya National Park boasts the largest known wild Asian elephant population at one place in the world. Many Sri Lanka tours include safaris as part of the package and provide the opportunity to see the country’s many highlights. 

India

India might not be your first thought when it comes to safari, but this incredible country offers visitors the chance to spot tigers in the wild. Ranthambore National Park in Northern India boasts a wide array of wildlife including leopards, hyenas, jackals, bears, bats, Indian flying foxes and mongoose- but it is most famous for its tiger population. A fantastic addition to any India tour, spotting a tiger in the wild deserves the crown of a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

Botswana

Botswana is a country with a healthy wildlife population and is home to the most prolific wetlands on the African continent. The unique landscape of the Okavango Delta, much of which is waterways and swamp, makes for an interesting alternative when it comes to a wildlife safari. Home to the Big Five and more, the Okavango Delta deservedly makes the UNESCO World Heritage List and unlike other African safaris, is best done by plane or boat.

posts.title: 10 Must-Try Foods in Italy
posts.content:
Italy is a country that has long been famed for its incredible food scene and should be the top of any foodie’s travel list. This is a country that takes the art of cooking and dining very seriously. So it’s little surprise that food plays a large role in most Italy holidays, with feasting on local delights a standard part of the daily agenda. Gastronomy is big business in Italy and you can expect to take your taste buds on a wonderful journey during your time in the country. To narrow things down a little, we’ve selected 10 must-try dishes that you cannot leave Italy without trying…

1.Pasta

Italy is a country that isn’t afraid of carbs and it is estimated that the average Italian eats over 60 pounds of pasta per year (around six times more than a UK citizen). Italians are more than happy to eat pasta on a daily basis and holidaymakers will find that freshly made pasta is widely available. If all your sightseeing gives you an appetite then treat yourself to a comforting bowl of spaghetti or creamy carbonara. For the more adventurous, pasta con le sarde (pasta with fresh sardines) is a must-try during a Sardinia getaway or Pappardelle al Cinghiale (ribbon pasta with wild boar) is a delicious addition to a Florence break.

2.Pizza

Developed in Naples in the late 18th century, pizza has long been a part of Western cuisine. However, Italians would agree that you haven’t had pizza until you’ve tried ‘authentic’ pizza in Italy itself. A must-try on any Naples holiday, Neapolitan pizza is cooked at a high temperature in a traditional wood-fired oven which makes for a mouth-wateringly charred crust contrasted with an elasticated dough. Soak up the flavours with a simple (but not to be discounted)Margherita pizza, or opt for a capriccioso, which is laden with mozzarella, Italian ham, artichoke and cremini mushrooms.

3.Gnocchi

Gnocchi is a delicious variety of pasta shaped into small, mouth-watering dumplings that are available with different toppings. Often eaten as first course in Italy (primo piatto), handmade gnocchi is light and fluffy, and a must-try on any Italian adventure! You’ll find gnocchi on most menus throughout Italy and popular toppings include sage and butter, tomato and fresh basil or pesto sauce- often all finished with parmesan.

4. Tiramisu

This Italian staple tastes of pure indulgence. A sumptuous mix of mascarpone, coffee, cocoa and liqueur, its invention is a hot-topic in Italy with many regions and cities claiming ownership. Known as a ‘pick-me-up’ this booze-laden dessert makes for an ideal end to a meal. You’ll find tiramisu on the menu throughout Italy; however, Venice, Rome, Florence and Naples are renowned for having very high standards in the tiramisu department. As well as in restaurants, you’ll also find gourmet cakes shops selling tiramisu in all major Italian cities.

5. Risotto alla Milanese

Northern Italy is world-famous for its risotto, and risotto alla Milanese with its golden colour and the saffron-infused flavour is extra special. A speciality in Milan, Risotto Alla Milanese, is often made with beef stock, bone marrow, lard and cheese before being flavoured with saffron. Throughout Italy, risotto is often served as a first course, however risotto alla Milanesehas been awarded main course status and is a popular dish with tourists enjoying a break in Milan. 

6. Pannacotta

Panna cotta (Italian for cooked cream) is an indulgent dessert that originates from Piedmont in northwest Italy. This creamy white dessert is super light and fluffy, comes in various flavours, and is often topped with a drizzle of caramel. The best places to sample panna cotta are in said to be in the nearby city of Turin, although you’ll find delicious versions throughout the country.

7. Tartufo

Gelato (ice-cream) is a big deal throughout Italy and in Calabria, and this multi-layered ice-cream bombe lays claim to being one of the best desserts in the world. Originating from Pizzo, Tarfutousually consists of two or more flavours of ice-cream with pureed fruit or liquid chocolate in the centre.The ice-cream is typically covered in a hard shell of chocolate or cocoa and flavoured with cinnamon and nuts.

8. Caponata

This famous Sicilian dish is a rich summer salad made from aubergine, tomatoes and other seasonal ingredients such as pine nuts and capers. The flavours are a wonderful blend of sweet and sour, with various different varieties available throughout Sicily. It can be served both hot and cold, as antipasto (first course), a side dish or as a meal itself, and if you’re on holiday in Sicily, it won’t be hard to find. 

9. Arancini

Also hailing from Sicily, arancini are stuffed rice balls that are coated in breadcrumbs and then fried until golden. Crispy on the outside and oozing with delicious fillings (usually involving cheese), these tasty bites are a must-try. Arancini are commonly eaten as a snack in Italy and can be found in many Sicilian cafes with various different fillings and sauces. 

10. Lavaret

Lavaret is a fresh-water fish found in the lakes of Northern Italy, and a popular dish with tourists on holiday in Lake Garda or Lake Como. Lavaret is a whitefish with a tender taste and is usually served baked, grilled or fried and seasoned with plenty of fresh garlic. Like many of Italy’s wonderful dishes, it’s all about fresh food with maximum flavour and minimum ingredients.

posts.title: Puglia holidays: food, hotels and things to do in Puglia, Italy
posts.content:
It’s unlikely you’ll be disappointed with a holiday in Puglia. This southern Italian region is lousy with beaches, caves, castles and a certain undeniable charm that stays with visitors long after returning home. Read on to find out all about this southern belle of Italy, including what to eat (from ‘orecchiette’ pasta to fluffy ‘friselle’ bread) and what to do in Puglia.

Where is Puglia?

Puglia (also known as Apulia) sits in the southeast in Italy’s ‘heel’– in reference to the country’s boot-like shape. The area boasts approximately 800km of coastline and enjoys a relatively dry climate, making it the perfect destination for sun-seekers.It is touched by the Adriatic Sea in the north and the Ionio in the south. 

Things to do in Puglia

There are so many beautiful pockets of Puglia, it’s hard to know where to begin. The towns of Ostuni and Otranto are spellbinding –like windows into the past – and there’s plenty of nature to discover, including cave complexes and national parks.

We’ve outlined some of our favourite things to do in Puglia below. Whether you’rea thrill seeker or beach bum, we hope this’ll give you plenty of food for thought when it comes to crafting your to-do list. 

Architecture

  • A trip to Puglia isn’t complete without a visit to the ‘trulli’ in Alberobello. A ‘trullo’ is a dry stone hut with a conical roof, native to the Puglia region. Listed as UNESCO sites, some of them date all the way back to the 14th century. 
  • Venture to Castel del Monte. This is a 13th-century citadel and castle built by Emperor Frederick II. It sits atop a hill in Andria and is made up of mighty beige blocks. 
  • Discover the Basilica San Nicola, a church located in Bari and an important pilgrimage destination for Catholics and Christians. 
  • Go to the historical Castello Normanno-Svevo, another beautiful castle in Bari with origins in the 12th century. 

Towns and villages

  • Discover the historic city of Ostuni and its whitewashed old town. Marvel at Ostuni Cathedral and step back in time at the Civic Museum. 
  • See beautiful Otranto, a coastal town that’s home to a 15th century Aragonese Castle and 11th century cathedral with mosaic flooring. There are also a range of beaches and two inland lakes. 
  • Visit Monte Sant’Angelo, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This town and ‘comune’ is home to the Sanctuary of Monte Sant’Angelo, the Giants’ Tower and the Tomb of Rothari. 
  • Visit the small fisherman’s village of Santa Maria Al Bagno. Boasting unspoiled beaches dotted along the coastline, it’s a great place to soak up the sun. You’ll also find restaurants, pubs, bars and dinky little shops and boutiques, allowing you to experience traditional Italian life at the fullest. 

Nature

  • Head deep into the stunning Grotte di Castellana, a karst cave complex in Bari.
  • Visit the awe-inspiring Gargano Peninsula, jutting out into the Adriatic and forming the ‘spur’ in the heel of Italy’s ‘boot’. It’s also home to Gargano National Park. 

Puglia beaches

  • Torre Guaceto, Brindisi – this is a protected marine area and nature reserve. A paradise for snorkelers, the waters are brimming with coral reefs and seagrass. 
  • BaiadeiTurchi, Otranto – characterised by pearly-white sand and azure waters, you can reach ‘the bay of the Turks’ by travelling on foot through a pine forest. 
  • Polignano a Mare, Bari – this popular beach has crystal-clear waters and acres of golden sand. 
  • Torre Pozzelle, Ostuni – this beach is rocky, wild and unspoiled. There’s plenty of lush vegetation and dramatic cliffs and coves. 
  • Torre dell’ Orso, Melendugno – overlooked by a 16th-century coastal tower, Torre dell’ Orsoboasts rolling sand dunes, sparkling sea and two twin rocks a few metres offshore, named the Two Sisters. 

Food in Puglia

Puglia boasts a wide variety of different cuisines and delicacies. As with most parts of Italy, pasta is the centrepiece of most meals (particularly ‘orecchiette’ which translates to ‘little ears’). Fish and seafood are also very popular here – which is unsurprising given the region’s proximity to two seas. 

Here are some examples of local food in Puglia:

  • Orecchiette al ragu di carne –‘orecchiette’ is a type of typical Puglian pasta. This dish is with a meat-based sauce.
  • Orecchiette allecime di rapa – a type of vegetarian pasta dish with turnip tops and other greens. 
  • Calzone – a popular street food that’s baked or deep-fried. It looks like a pizza that has been folded over. 
  • Cavatelli con le cozze –a pastadishwithmussels.
  • Risotto aifrutti di mare – seafood risotto.
  • Friselle– a type of Puglian bread with tomatoes, basil and oil.

Package holidays to Puglia

Many of our Italy holiday offers include flights, accommodation and transfers – taking the hassle out of booking and leaving you to focus on how you want to spend your time at your destination. 

When it comes to package holidays to Puglia, choose from one of the following deals:

- A full-board Southern Italy break at a four-star hotel

- A seven-night all-inclusive stay in Puglia


Useful information

  • In Puglia, the language spoken is Italian. 
  • The currency is euros. 
  • The time difference is GMT+1. 
  • The power plugs and sockets are type L with two or three round prongs. You’ll need an adapter. 
  • 113 is the national emergency number. 

At Flights and Packages, we not only offer package holidays to Puglia but also plenty of other jam-packed tours and trips too. Fuel your wanderlust and have a look at our other European city breaks here.

posts.title: Holidays in Thailand: Top 10 Must-Try Dishes
posts.content:
Renowned for its incredible landscape, Thailand is also famed for its flavoursome cuisine, and Thai food plays a large part when it comes to Holidays in Thailand. Fresh and fragrant, Thai cuisine blends various different ingredients to create unique flavours in various healthy dishes. Whether you’re relaxing on an island, exploring the sights in bustling Bangkok or getting back to nature in northern Thailand, there is a myriad of flavours, restaurant dishes and street food for you to sample. With so many dishes to choose from it can be hard to know where to start, so using our expert Thailand knowledge, we’ve chosen 10 must-try dishes that are nothing short of delicious… 

1. Green Chicken Curry (Gaeng Keow Wan Kai)

A popular choice in UK restaurants, many believe you have not tried green chicken curry until you’ve tried it in its homeland. This mouth-watering, aromatic dish is often spicier in Thailand and combines chunks of chicken, Thai eggplant, bamboo shoots, coriander and basil with coconut milk, green chilli, kaffir lime, galangal, shrimp paste, coriander, lemongrass, and even more spices! Often served with fragrant jasmine rice, you’ll find this delicious dish all over Thailand, from high-end hotels to the street-food markets in Bangkok.

2. Red Curry (Gaeng Daeng)

Also a popular dish in the UK, this delicious curry is made with chicken, beef, pork, duck, shrimp, or tofu in Thailand, and is less spicy than Thai green curry (the reverse is true in the UK). Ingredients include garlic, shallots, chilli peppers, galangal, fish sauce and kaffir lime leaves, and like green curry, the dish is popular throughout the whole of Thailand. 

3. Thai Style Fried Noodles (Pad Thai)

This iconic noodle dish can be found throughout much of Thailand and is a popular offering with street-food vendors in Bangkok. Undeniably more flavoursome than the UK version, this stir-fried rice noodles dish comes with various different proteins and is created using chilli, fish sauce, ground peanuts, sugar and lime juice. You’ll find pad thai on most menus and many consider it a must-try during holidays to Thailand.

4. Mango Sticky Rice (Khao Niaow Ma Muang)

Deliciously sweet, yet healthy, this tasty treat is a fantastic way to finish off a spicy Thai meal. Alternatively, it can also be eaten as a snack and you’ll find it at street stalls, as well as high-end establishments. The dish consists of perfectly ripe, succulent mango chunks beside sticky rice that has been drenched in coconut cream. It’s often served in banana leaves and topped with crunchy sesame seeds. Be warned - it’s seriously moreish! 

5. Chiang Mai Curried Noodles (Khao Soi)

Popular in northern Thailand and a must-try if you are on a Thailand tour to Chiang Mai, Khao Soi is a soup made with yellow curry and coconut milk and served with both boiled and fried noodles. The satisfying soup usually comes with chicken or beef, however, modern Chiang Mai restaurants also offer seafood or pork. The soup makes a delicious lunch before an afternoon of exploring. 

6. Spicy Green Papaya Salad (Som Tam)

Originating from northeast Thailand, this light, summery dish consists of shredded green papaya, ground chilli, tomatoes, garlic and green beans. The dish is complemented with fish sauce, lime, sugar, and peanuts, which create a spicy, sweet and sour flavour. Eat with sticky rice for a more substantial meal.

7. Spicy Shrimp Soup (Tom Yum Goong)

Rich in vitamin A, this hot and sour soup packs a punch in the flavour department. You’ll find slightly different versions throughout Thailand, and it’s a popular dish in Bangkok restaurants. Common ingredients include shrimp, shrimp stock, Thai chillies, Thai roasted chilli paste, fish sauce and mushrooms. Many eateries in Bangkok add evaporated milk to the recipe for an extra creamy soup. 

8. Spicy Beef Salad (Yum Nua)

This refreshing salad ticks a lot of boxes - healthy, flavoursome and a perfect match for the hot Thai weather. Topped with strips of tender beef, this salad comes with a mouth-watering dressing made lime juice, sesame oil, soy sauce, ginger, fish sauce, sugar and garlic. Like spicy green papaya salads, yum nua is often eaten with sticky rice for a filling, flavoursome meal. 

9. Chicken with Cashew Nuts (Kai Med Ma Muang)

A popular dish with UK tourists, this tasty dish gets its sweet, sour and tangy flavour from fish sauce, brown sugar, soy sauce and dried red chillies. The chicken often comes with a slightly crispy coating and the dish goes perfectly with sticky Thai rice. You can order this in most of Thailand’s major tourist spots.

10. Fried Rice (Khao Pad)

This simple yet delicious dish never disappoints. Available at almost every street-food market and made with jasmine rice, this hearty dish is often flavoured with onions, garlic, egg, fish sauce, lime and soy sauce, chillies and coriander. Additions include sweet pork, fresh shrimps and chicken.

posts.title: Sicily Holidays: food, weather and things to do in Sicily
posts.content:
A sun-baked region of Italy, Sicily boasts a pleasant climate, to-die-for beaches and age-old ruins – a real feast for the senses. Discover more about this island with our guide to its climate, food and beaches and become one step closer to booking your Sicily holiday.

Where is Sicily?

The largest Italian region, Sicily is an island sitting just off the “toe” of the country’s“boot”. It is separated from the mainland by the Straits of Messina and is also home to Mount Etna, one of Europe’s tallest active volcanoes. 

Sicily weather

Typical Sicilian summers are hot and dry, while the winters are warm and rainy. The southeast is typically drier and warmer than the northwest and the areas at a higher altitude are generally cooler. 

The rugged, mountainous terrain of the island means that the climate varies from region to region but generally speaking, the island enjoys warm temperatures all year round. 

In summer (June to September) the average temperature can rise to 26 degrees and the water temperature can even reach 27 degrees in August. Meanwhile, winter is mild with highs of 12 or 13 degrees and lows of five degrees. 

Things to do in Sicily

With so many interesting towns, time-ravaged ruins, castles and glorious beaches, there are plenty of things to do in Sicily to suit every type of traveller – whether you’re a culture vulture, beach fan or history buff. 

We’ve put together a collection of things to do in Sicily below. 

Towns and cities

  • Agrigento – Agrigento is home to the Valley of the Temples, the most famous archaeological site in all of Sicily. Here, marvel at eight temples including the well-preserved Temple of Concorde and Temple of Hera. 
  • Palermo – Palermo is the island’s capital. Its must-sees include the Norman Palace, Palermo Cathedral, Massimo Theatre and the Ballaro Market. 
  • Catania – sitting at the foot of Mount Etna, Catania is Sicily’s second-largest city and has buildings built from lava stone – giving it the nickname “the black city”. The Piazza del Duomo and the Duomo di Catania are two of the highlights. 
  • Taormina – known as “the Pearl of Sicily”, Taormina is home to Isola Bella (a small island and beach), a Greek Theatre and the Madonna della Rocca Church. 
  • Messina – a harbour city in the northeast that’s known for its Norman Messina Cathedral and marble fountains (Fontana di Orione and Neptune Fountain). 
  • Syracuse – a modern city on the east coast of the island, Syracuse’s primary attractions are the Neapolis archaeological park, Roman amphitheatre and Greek theatre. 
  • Noto – listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Noto is bursting with palaces, churches and Baroque-style cathedrals. 
  • Erice – a small medieval town sitting on top of a rocky cliff. It has a stunning castle, 14th-century medieval church and labyrinthine medieval lanes. 
  • Aeolian Islands – these islands are located in the north of the island. There are seven in total but the most popular are Lipari, Salina, Vulcano and Stromboli. 

Nature

  • Go on an excursion to the crater of Mount Etna – an impressive active stratovolcano. It’s Europe’s largest active volcano and experiences frequent eruptions. 
  • Visit the Zingaro Nature Reserve – Sicily’s most famous nature reserve with panoramic views and picturesque coves. 
  • Marvel at the Selinunte Temples – an archaeological park with several beautiful temples to marvel at. 
  • Venture to the salt flats in Trapani. These are dried-up desert lakes where the salt is piled up in pyramids. Pink flamingos also congregate here. 

Sicily beaches

Sicily beaches certainly do not disappoint. Lose yourself in breath-taking shoreline scenery and paddle in balmy azure waters. 

  • Isola Bella – located at the bottom of Taormina, it’s a small pebble beach with crystal-clear waters and lots of beautiful marine life – a great place for snorkelling. 
  • Calamosche Beach – sitting by Syracuse, it has shallow, pleasant waters. 
  • Scala deiTurchi – a romantic beach where you can enjoy the sunset. It has a white stair-like limestone cliff that contrasts vibrantly with the turquoise ocean before it. 
  • CalaRossa in Favignana – a glistening cove with turquoise waters, surrounded by large rocks. 
  • San Vito Lo Capo Beach – a large beach with plenty of sand, overlooked by Mount Monaco. 
  • Torre Salsa – this is a nature reserve frequented by loggerhead sea turtles that come to breed. It is managed by the World Wide Fund for Nature. 

Sicilian food

Given its location, seafood is a firm feature in much of Sicily’s cuisine. In addition, tomatoes and olive oil are used frequently(instead of cream and butter which are more typically used in northern parts of Italy). 

Sicilian desserts are also exceptional. We recommend trying “cannoli”(cylindrical pastries with cream or cheese inside) and “cassata” (a round sponge cake with fruit juices/liqueur). 

Some other examples of typical Sicilian food are:

  • Arancini – balls of deep-fried risotto rice. 
  • Gamberorosso – fresh red prawns. Dress them in a little lemon juice and olive oil and eat them raw. 
  • Pasta alla Norma – made using tomatoes, aubergines, garlic, basil and salted ricotta, it’s one of the island’s most famous pasta dishes. 
  • Pasta con le sarde – a pasta dish which uses sardines. 
  • Limoncello – a lemon liqueur typical of the region.

Package holidays to Sicily

Many of our Sicily holiday offers include flights, accommodation and transfers – taking the hassle out of booking and leaving you to focus on how you want to spend your time at your destination. 

Depending on how long you want to go for and whether or not you’d like all meals included, Flights and Packages have a few package holidays to Sicily:

  • 3-night Palermo city break
  • 7-night half-board getaway
  • 5-night all-inclusive package deal

Useful information

  • In Sicily, the language spoken is Italian. 
  • The currency is euros. 
  • The time difference is GMT+1. 
  • The power plugs and sockets are type L with two or three round prongs. You’ll need an adapter. 
  • 113 is the national emergency number. 

At Flights and Packages, we not only offer package holidays to Sicily but also plenty of other jam-packed tours and trips too. Fuel your wanderlust and have a look at our other European city breaks here.

posts.title: Sorrento holidays: weather, beaches, food and things to do in Sorrento, Italy
posts.content:
Sorrento holidays: weather, beaches, food and things to do in Sorrento, Italy

A coastal town nestled serenely on top of a cliffside, Sorrento is known for its dazzling views. It’s also a great stepping stone for visiting other southern Italian must-sees, including the picturesque Amalfi Coast, the glamorous island of Capri and historic Pompeii. 

Its architecture is beautiful and varied. With 14th century churches and 11th-century cathedrals, exploring Sorrento is like stepping back in time. 

To give you a better understanding of what to expect from a Sorrento holiday, we’ve put together a guide with all the things you need to know – including what to eat, what to see and what to do in Sorrento. 

Where is Sorrento?

Sorrento is a town on the southwest coast of Italy in the region of Campania. Sitting on top of cliffs and overlooking the Bay of Naples, flight time to Sorrento is just over two and a half hours from the UK. 

Sorrento weather

Sorrento enjoys a typical Mediterranean climate with hot and dry summers. Temperatures are highest in July and August with highs of 29 degrees and lows of 18 degrees, while the coldest month is January with highs of 12 and lows of 4 degrees. 

Springtime sees warm, pleasant weather and the lemon trees (which the area is famous for) are in bloom at this time, making for wonderful scenery.

Things to do in Sorrento

Whether you’re keen to get a taste of the Sorrentine way of life or are simply using the town as a springboard for venturing to other destinations, there are plenty of things to do in Sorrento to suit every type of traveller. 

Architecture

  • Sorrento Cathedral – step foot in Sorrento’s “Duomo”, a Roman Catholic construction and first erected in the 11th century. 
  • The Church of St Francis – this was founded by Saint Antoninus, the Patron Saint of Sorrento. It was transformed into a church in the 14th century and currently has a marble white façade. 
  • Basilica of St Antoninus – the town’s oldest church. 
  • Villa Pollio Felice – these are ruins of a first-century Roman villa on Punta del Capo, boasting superlative views of the ocean. 
  • Museo Correale di Terranova – spread out over three floors, this museum has collections from Neapolitan painters dating back to the 17th and 18th centuries, as well as ceramics, glassware, crystals and watches. 
  • Archaeological Museum of the Sorrentine Peninsula – this museum has all sorts of local treasures. Located on top of a cliff overlooking Naples Bay, it’s also a great place to take photos of the breath-taking scenery. 

Places

  • Piazza Tasso – wander this central square, named after the poet Torquato Tasso. Home to the Baroque Church del Carmine, it is also a gateway to the shopping street of Via San Cesareo. 
  • Marina Grande – stroll around this pastel-coloured fishing village and stop in at one of the family-run seafood restaurants that line the shore. 
  • Sorrento Old Town – meander through the bustling Old Town with its colourful, pastel buildings and thrumming marketplaces. 
  • Capri – sitting just a stone’s throw from the Sorrentine Peninsula (20 minutes by boat to be exact) is the captivating island of Capri, a paradise with five-star hotels and sensational scenery. 
  • Pompeii – Sorrento is only a short drive from Pompeii and Mount Vesuvius. Take a day trip to this historically-rich and interesting area if you have the time. 

Nature

  • Lemon groves – lemons from Sorrento are some of the best in Italy. Tour one of the groves and make sure you sample limoncello during your stay. 
  • Punta Campanella – this nature reserve links the Sorrentine Peninsula with the Amalfi Coast. The area is steeped in history; according to legend, it is where Ulysses met the Sirens.
  • Villa Comunale – stroll around this large park with fountains, gardens and beautiful scenery. 

Sorrento beaches

Though Sorrento isn’t known for its beaches due to its hilltop position, you can still find some lovely, picturesque spots for bathing and basking in the Italian sunshine. 

Here are some of our recommended beaches in Sorrento:

  • Marina Grande Beach – Sorrento’s fishing village has a small volcanic sand beach. 
  • Marina Piccola Beach – a smaller strip of volcanic sand that’s nice for swimming. 
  • Puolo – an area with a large stretch of public beach and shallow waters, perfect for families. 
  • Marina del Cantone – this bay sits near the area of Nerano. Boasting a large pebble beach with sparkling waters, it is also a great place to try the local speciality “spaghetti allaNerano” made with provolone cheese and zucchini. 
  • Leonelli’s Beach – this square-shaped beach also has a fairly large restaurant area, meaning you can try typical Sorrentine dishes if you want. 

Sorrento food

Aside from the typical pizza and pasta dishes which Italy is known for, there are a few culinary staples native to the area of Sorrento that we recommend you try during your stay: 

  • Gnocchi alla Sorrentina– gnocchi pasta with tomato sauce, mozzarella and basil.
  • Scialatelli – a type of pasta that’s short, thick and linguine-like. It originated from the Amalfi coast. 
  • Sfogliatella – a shell-shaped Italian pastry from the Campania region. It is sometimes referred to as “lobster tail” in English. 
  • Pastiera – a Neapolitan tart made from wheat, eggs and ricotta cheese. 
  • Limoncello – a digestive alcoholic liqueur made from lemon peel. Produced mainly in the south of Italy, this is how locals round off their meals.  

Package holidays to Sorrento

Many of our Sorrento holiday offers include flights, accommodation and transfers – taking the hassle out of booking and leaving you to focus on how you want to spend your time at your destination. 

Depending on how long you want to go for and whether or not you’d like to combine your tour with other destinations, Flights and Packages have a wealth of package holidays to Sorrento to fuel your wanderlust:

  • 4 nights at a 4-star with breakfast and transfers
  • 4 nights at a 4-star on all-inclusive board
  • 5 nights at a 4-star with Pompeii and Vesuvius tour
  • 7-night twin-centre to Rome and Sorrento
  • 7-night twin-centre to Capri and Sorrento
  • 8-night triple centre to Venice, Rome and Sorrento

Useful information

  • In Sorrento, the language spoken is Italian. 
  • The currency is euros. 
  • The time difference is GMT+1. 
  • The power plugs and sockets are type L with two or three round prongs. You’ll need an adapter. 
  • 113 is the national emergency number. 

At Flights and Packages, we not only offer package holidays to Sorrento but also plenty of other exciting excursion-packed trips too. Have a look at our other European city breaks here and book a much-deserved getaway for 2020!
